query: You are working on the thunderbird/thunderbird-android repository. The codebase is at version 0.1 (commit: a2c9b0ac). ## Issue to Resolve The recipient address is not a valid RFC-5321 address. ### Checklist - [X] I have used the search function to see if someone else has already submitted the same bug report. - [X] I will describe the problem with as much detail as possible. ### App version v6.602 ### Where did you get the app from? Google Play ### Android version 13 ### Device model Moto X40 ### Steps to reproduce Edit a mail, input the recipient address end with a `/`, then send it. https://github.com/thundernest/k-9/assets/10363352/cbb7a99a-b348-4f88-adc3-3fccc1f4ba29 ### Expected behavior We can check the mail address before sending it, toast a message to tell the user that the address is invalid, or show a dialog like Gmail did below: ### Actual behavior Sending it, and showing the failure. ### Logs _No response_ ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `legacy/ui/legacy/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/view/UserInputEmailAddressParser.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

content.text:
Now I can see the issue. The `getHostname()` method returns the part after the `@` sign. For `xxx@gmail.com/`, it returns `gmail.com/` which is not null or blank, so `isIncomplete()` returns false. The address passes through without validation.

The fix should add email address validation in `UserInputEmailAddressParser` to reject addresses with invalid characters. Let me look at what a valid email address looks like and check if there's an existing validation pattern.

1â†’package com.fsck.k9
 2â†’
 3â†’import java.util.regex.Pattern
 4â†’
 5â†’class EmailAddressValidator {
 6â†’
 7â†’    fun isValidAddressOnly(text: CharSequence): Boolean = EMAIL_ADDRESS_PATTERN.matcher(text).matches()
 8â†’
 9â†’    companion object {
10â†’
11â†’        // https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt (3.2.2)
12â†’        // https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5321.txt (4.1.2)
13â†’
14â†’        private const val ALPHA = "[a-zA-Z]"
15â†’        private const val ALPHANUM = "[a-zA-Z0-9]"
16â†’        private const val ATEXT = "[0-9a-zA-Z!#$%&'*+\\-/=?^_`{|}~]"
17â†’        private const val QCONTENT = "([\\p{Graph}\\p{Blank}&&[^\"\\\\]]|\\\\[\\p{Graph}\\p{Blank}])"
18â†’        private const val TOP_LABEL = "(($ALPHA($ALPHANUM|\\-|_)*$ALPHANUM)|$ALPHA)"
19â†’        private const val DOMAIN_LABEL = "(($ALPHANUM($ALPHANUM|\\-|_)*$ALPHANUM)|$ALPHANUM)"
20â†’        private const val HOST_NAME = "((($DOMAIN_LABEL\\.)+$TOP_LABEL)|$DOMAIN_LABEL)"
21â†’
22â†’        private val EMAIL_ADDRESS_PATTERN = Pattern.compile(
23â†’            "^($ATEXT+(\\.$ATEXT+)*|\"$QCONTENT+\")" +
24â†’                "\\@$HOST_NAME",
25â†’        )
26â†’    }
27â†’}
28â†’

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. When a user enters `xxx@gmail.com/`, the `Address` class parses it and `getHostname()` returns `gmail.com/` (not null/blank), so `isIncomplete()` returns false. Then it goes to `Address.parse()` which uses the mime4j parser. The mime4j parser may handle this badly.

The fix should validate the email address using the existing `EmailAddressValidator` in the `UserInputEmailAddressParser`. An address like `xxx@gmail.com/` has an invalid character `/` in the domain, so it should be rejected.

Let me check how `EmailAddressValidator` handles this case and see how to integrate it:

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:

13â†’        // Most of the tests based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Email_address#Examples
14â†’        assertValidAddress("simple@example.com")
15â†’        assertValidAddress("very.common@example.com")
16â†’        assertValidAddress("disposable.style.email.with+symbol@example.com")
17â†’        assertValidAddress("other.email-with-hyphen@example.com")
18â†’        assertValidAddress("fully-qualified-domain@example.com")
19â†’        assertValidAddress("user.name+tag+sorting@example.com")
20â†’        assertValidAddress("example-indeed@strange-example.com")

22â†’        assertValidAddress("example@1.com")

25â†’        assertValidAddress("\"very.(),:;<>[]\\\".VERY.\\\"very@\\\\ \\\"very\\\".unusual\"@strange.example.com")
26â†’        assertValidAddress("\"()<>[]:,;@\\\\\\\"!#$%&'-/=?^_`{}| ~.a\"@example.org")
27â†’        assertValidAddress("\" \"@example.org")

29â†’

34â†’        assertInvalidAddress("this\\ still\\\"not\\\\allowed@example.com")

45â†’    }

The `EmailAddressValidator` would reject `xxx@gmail.com/` because `/` is not in the valid domain characters. Now I need to integrate the validation into `UserInputEmailAddressParser`. The approach should be to add a check that filters out addresses that don't match the email address pattern.

content.text:
Now I need to add validation. The key insight: when `xxx@gmail.com/` is entered, it gets parsed, `isIncomplete()` returns false (hostname is `gmail.com/`), `isNonAsciiAddress()` returns false (it's ASCII), and then `Address.parse()` processes it. The mime4j parser in `Address.parse()` might produce a broken address like `xxx@gmail.com` (stripping the slash) or it could fail.

The fix should add a validation check for invalid addresses. I'll add a check using `EmailAddressValidator`:

content.text:
The fix is complete. Here's a summary of the change:

**Root Cause:** When a user enters an email address like `xxx@gmail.com/`, the `UserInputEmailAddressParser` would:
1. Parse it via `Address.parseUnencoded()` â€” this succeeds, producing an Address with `gmail.com/` as the hostname
2. Check `isIncomplete()` â€” returns `false` because hostname (`gmail.com/`) is not null/blank
3. Check `isNonAsciiAddress()` â€” returns `false` because it's all ASCII
4. Pass it to `Address.parse()` which would either produce a broken address or fail later during sending

**Fix:** Added a validation step using the existing `EmailAddressValidator` class (which implements RFC-5321 validation via a regex pattern). The new check `!emailAddressValidator.isValidAddressOnly(address.address)` is placed after the incomplete and non-ASCII checks but before the final `Address.parse()` call. If the address doesn't match the RFC-5321 pattern, it returns `null` (filtering it out), which prevents invalid addresses like `xxx@gmail.com/` from being accepted.

1â†’package com.fsck.k9
 2â†’
 3â†’import java.util.regex.Pattern
 4â†’
 5â†’class EmailAddressValidator {
 6â†’
 7â†’    fun isValidAddressOnly(text: CharSequence): Boolean = EMAIL_ADDRESS_PATTERN.matcher(text).matches()
 8â†’
 9â†’    companion object {
10â†’
11â†’        // https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt (3.2.2)
12â†’        // https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5321.txt (4.1.2)
13â†’
14â†’        private const val ALPHA = "[a-zA-Z]"
15â†’        private const val ALPHANUM = "[a-zA-Z0-9]"
16â†’        private const val ATEXT = "[0-9a-zA-Z!#$%&'*+\\-/=?^_`{|}~]"
17â†’        private const val QCONTENT = "([\\p{Graph}\\p{Blank}&&[^\"\\\\]]|\\\\[\\p{Graph}\\p{Blank}])"
18â†’        private const val TOP_LABEL = "(($ALPHA($ALPHANUM|\\-|_)*$ALPHANUM)|$ALPHA)"
19â†’        private const val DOMAIN_LABEL = "(($ALPHANUM($ALPHANUM|\\-|_)*$ALPHANUM)|$ALPHANUM)"
20â†’        private const val HOST_NAME = "((($DOMAIN_LABEL\\.)+$TOP_LABEL)|$DOMAIN_LABEL)"
21â†’
22â†’        private val EMAIL_ADDRESS_PATTERN = Pattern.compile(
23â†’            "^($ATEXT+(\\.$ATEXT+)*|\"$QCONTENT+\")" +
24â†’                "\\@$HOST_NAME",
25â†’        )
26â†’    }
27â†’}

 8â†’class EmailAddressValidatorTest {
 9â†’    private val validator = EmailAddressValidator()
10â†’
11â†’    @Test
12â†’    fun testEmailValidation() {
13â†’        // Most of the tests based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Email_address#Examples
14â†’        assertValidAddress("simple@example.com")
15â†’        assertValidAddress("very.common@example.com")
16â†’        assertValidAddress("disposable.style.email.with+symbol@example.com")
17â†’        assertValidAddress("other.email-with-hyphen@example.com")
18â†’        assertValidAddress("fully-qualified-domain@example.com")
19â†’        assertValidAddress("user.name+tag+sorting@example.com")
20â†’        assertValidAddress("example-indeed@strange-example.com")
21â†’        assertValidAddress("example-indeed@strange_example.com")
22â†’        assertValidAddress("example@1.com")
23â†’        assertValidAddress("admin@mailserver1")
24â†’        assertValidAddress("user@localserver")
25â†’        assertValidAddress("\"very.(),:;<>[]\\\".VERY.\\\"very@\\\\ \\\"very\\\".unusual\"@strange.example.com")
26â†’        assertValidAddress("\"()<>[]:,;@\\\\\\\"!#$%&'-/=?^_`{}| ~.a\"@example.org")
27â†’        assertValidAddress("\" \"@example.org")
28â†’        assertValidAddress("x@example.com")
29â†’
30â†’        assertInvalidAddress("Abc.example.com")
31â†’        assertInvalidAddress("\"not\"right@example.com")
32â†’        assertInvalidAddress("john.doe@example..com")
33â†’        assertInvalidAddress("example@c.2")
34â†’        assertInvalidAddress("this\\ still\\\"not\\\\allowed@example.com")
35â†’        assertInvalidAddress("john..doe@example.com")
36â†’        assertInvalidAddress("invalidperiod.@example.com")
37â†’    }
38â†’
39â†’    private fun assertValidAddress(input: String) {
40â†’        assertThat(input).transform { validator.isValidAddressOnly(it) }.isTrue()
41â†’    }
42â†’
43â†’    private fun assertInvalidAddress(input: String) {
44â†’        assertThat(input).transform { validator.isValidAddressOnly(it) }.isFalse()
45â†’    }
46â†’}
